papers.title: First-principles framework for exchange interactions and red-shifted luminescence of ${\mathrm{Cr}}^{3+}$ ion pairs in strong crystal field environments
papers.authors: Qinshi Hu, Lingkun Zhang, Anfei Chen, Qianshan Quan, Min Yin, Chang-Kui Duan, and Qiaoling Chen
papers.abstract: Author(s): Qinshi Hu, Lingkun Zhang, Anfei Chen, Qianshan Quan, Min Yin, Chang-Kui Duan, and Qiaoling ChenHere, the authors establish a first-principles framework, benchmarked against ruby (Al2O3:Cr3+) and applied to multiple oxides, for quantitatively determining the ground-state exchange coupling J and emission redshift of Cr3+ ion pairs in strong crystal-field environments. Redshifts increase along corner, edge, and face-sharing from less than 0.1 to ∼0.2 eV. The study shows that excited-state electron-phonon coupling and magnetic exchange work synergistically, and these geometry-specific redshift ranges can distinguish genuine pair-related near-infrared centers from other defects.[Phys. papers.title: From single-particle to many-body chaos in the Yukawa-Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model: Theory and a cavity-QED proposal
papers.authors: David Pascual Solis, Alex Windey, Soumik Bandyopadhyay, Andrea Legramandi, and Philipp Hauke
papers.abstract: Author(s): David Pascual Solis, Alex Windey, Soumik Bandyopadhyay, Andrea Legramandi, and Philipp HaukeUnderstanding transitions from integrable to chaotic regimes is a central challenge in quantum many-body systems. Here, combining perturbative and numerical analyses, the authors study how the Yukawa-Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model interpolates between integrable and chaotic regimes. By tuning the interaction strength, they reveal a finite-size crossover in spectral statistics and out-of-time-ordered correlators, marked by prethermal plateaus and incomplete scrambling. The proposed cavity-QED realization provides an experimentally accessible platform to probe the onset of chaos and thermalization in strongly interacting quantum matter.[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184121] Published Fri May 29, 2026

Gvasaliya, and A. ZheludevThe S=3/2 compound Cs2CoI4 is a new member of the celebrated family of frustrated magnets Cs2MX4 (M = transition metal ion; X = halogen or oxygen). Thermodynamic and neutron scattering measurements reveal a complex magnetic phase diagram and excitation spectrum markedly different from related compounds. A structural phase transition is central to understanding this behavior. Based on the solved low-temperature crystal structure, a spin Hamiltonian is constructed that captures the observed spin dynamics and provides a qualitative understanding of the phase diagram.[Phys.
